title: One small daily pleasure
subtitle: A five-part daily ritual built around one chosen pleasure, non-negotiable, repeated for a week.
excerpt: A five-part daily ritual built around one chosen pleasure: a candle, a cup of coffee in a particular cup, two pages of a book, a song in the kitchen. Same pleasure for seven days, same time of day, same shape. Five minutes; the same five minutes, claimed.
sourceNotes: The five-part Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor ritual structure is Rebecca J Page's framework, used across all twelve weekly ceremonies in The Money Journal (2025). The daily-pleasure theme is original to the homemade.education library.

content.text: A five-minute daily ritual built around one small chosen pleasure. The pleasure is named at the start of the week (a particular cup, a candle, two pages of a book) and the same pleasure is given the same five minutes for seven days. The ritual shape is consistent; only the pleasure varies.

One chosen pleasure for the week. Pick something small. A coffee in the green cup, the candle in the bathroom, the radio on a particular station for one song, two pages of a book before bed. Daily-life-sized, repeatable, the same each day.
Set the pleasure up. Pour the coffee, light the candle, open the book. Sit somewhere you can stay for five minutes. Phone down, door closed if possible.

attrs.quote: I am ready to release the rule that pleasure has to be earned. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow pleasure as a daily need. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

content.text: Take the pleasure. Drink the coffee slow. Read the two pages without skimming. Watch the candle for a minute. Don't multitask. The pleasure is the practice; doing it alongside the email is doing something else.
When the five minutes are up, sit for one more breath. Note that the practice happened.
Put the cup away, blow out the candle, close the book. The ritual is closed.

The five-part shape (Prepare, Release, Allow, Integrate, Anchor) is the ritual frame Rebecca uses in The Money Journal. The daily-pleasure theme is new. Built for homemade.education, it sets one small chosen pleasure, done daily for a week, as a real ritual rather than a luxury.
